lesson.canDo: By the end you can say:
lesson.peak: Stand up, smile, use a big voice!
lesson.ministoryLead: Hear the question → answer OUT LOUD → check.
lesson.transcript.replay: Use the player's ⟲10s to replay a part
lesson.grammar.sayTrue: Say a true sentence about yourself (out loud)
lesson.grammar.nice: Great! Saying it aloud is what matters most.
drill.done: Well done! You've gone through all the questions.
lesson.pov.tenseVal: present perfect (since…) → past simple (a single event)
lesson.pov.noTts: Read-aloud isn't available in this browser — read the text yourself.
lesson.ep.predict: Make a prediction, then listen to the dialogue.
lesson.ep.listenCold: Listen cold first — get the gist.
lesson.ep.explain: Listen to the hosts' explanation (skippable on Sprint).
lesson.ep.shadowHint: Speak along with the audio, about half a second behind — copy the melody.
lesson.ep.roleplayHint: Hide one role and read it aloud yourself. Role A first, then B.
lesson.ep.recap: Review (rv) — reinforce the words once more.
lesson.six.quizLead: Pre-listening question — predict first, then listen.
lesson.six.listen: Listen once without the transcript, then reveal the answer.
lesson.six.insert: ⭐ Pay attention to the INSERT clips — the hardest, most useful accent stretch (authentic voices).
lesson.six.speakit: This topic feeds the Speak-It section — record a 60-second answer.
lesson.fun.body: Sit back and watch — a fun video on this topic keeps your ear tuned to natural, real speech.
lesson.fun.watchTask: Watch it and write down 3 new words.
lesson.fun.blocked: The video wouldn't open here. Open it directly on YouTube:
lesson.fun.watchedMsg: Nice! Relaxed watching sharpens your ear too.
speak.instructionDefault: Speak for about 60 seconds. Use this week's two grammar topics.
speak.privacy: Nothing is uploaded — your recording stays on this device, only with you.
speak.answerAloud: No mic? Still answer the question OUT LOUD — it helps a lot even without recording.
speak.hook: Keep your Lesson 1 recording and listen again at Lesson 30 — you'll HEAR how far you've come!
speak.enough: That's enough — stop whenever you're ready.
speak.confirmRerecord: The new recording will replace the old one. Continue?
speak.deniedHow: Tap the lock 🔒 or microphone icon in your browser's address bar, allow the microphone for this site, then try again.
speak.unsupported: This browser doesn't support voice recording. Answer the question above out loud instead.
speak.noPersist: Note: the recording couldn't be saved on this device (browser setting) — you can only play it back this session.
speak.noMic: No microphone found. Connect a microphone and try again.
check.gateReason: First answer the mini-story questions out loud (at least 2 times).
check.laterS5: Full completion and the star are enabled in a later slice (S5).
check.needMore: Finish grammar A+B, vocabulary, and MAIN ×3.
check.need.main: Listen to the MAIN talk 3 times.
check.need.pov: Listen to the POV story 2 times.
check.praise: Brilliant! You spoke aloud — that's what matters most. 🎉

lesson.ep.transcriptHint: Read the dialogue transcript and study the Key Vocabulary (with the Uzbek gloss).
lesson.ep.shadowDoneMsg: Great — shadowing builds your rhythm and pronunciation.
lesson.ep.sprintSkip: Sprint pace: you can skip this
lesson.ep.stepHint: Shadow, then play one role out loud — that completes the EnglishPod step (2★).
lesson.ep.epDone: EnglishPod done (shadow + a role) — counts toward 2★.
lesson.six.predictCue: Made your guess? Now listen and check.
lesson.six.revealLead: After listening, reveal the answer and self-check.
lesson.six.packReviewedMsg: Nice — these 6 words are the topic's key vocabulary.
lesson.six.stepHint: Answer the quiz and review the 6 words — that completes the 6 Minute English step (3★).
lesson.six.sixDone: 6 Minute English done (quiz + words) — counts toward 3★.

set.importInvalid: This doesn't look like valid YouStudy - English data.
set.previewWarn: This will REPLACE all progress currently on this device.
set.importFail: Import failed — your data is unchanged.

ielts.lead: How this course lines up with CEFR levels and the IELTS exam — honestly.
ielts.honestTitle: This is not a cram course
ielts.honestBody: This course builds the real spoken competence that IELTS measures — fluency, vocabulary, grammar and listening. It is not an exam-cram course. Once you are at B1 and above, add task-specific practice on top: timing, the band descriptors, full timed mock tests, and Reading & Writing. The grammar spine here runs B1 → B2.
ielts.p1Speaking: Part 1 foundations: self, family, routine, food, study — short answers with no hesitation.
ielts.p1Listening: Section 1: everyday social and transactional talk (the EnglishPod dialogues).
ielts.p2Speaking: Extended Part 1 and the start of Part 2: narrate the past, state plans, give advice, talk about experiences.
ielts.p2Listening: Section 2: monologue on everyday topics (EnglishPod programs + 6 Minute English).
ielts.p3Speaking: Full Part 2 long turn and Part 3 discussion: range and accuracy, plus the Interview-Skills mock bridge.
ielts.p3Listening: Sections 3–4: multi-speaker and academic monologue, with fast, varied accents (the 6ME INSERT clips).
ielts.critFluencyWhere: Answering mini-story questions aloud builds automaticity; connectors, concession and discourse markers across the lessons; then your own 60-second recorded talks.
ielts.critLexicalWhere: Vocabulary glossaries, EnglishPod Key Vocab and the 6-word packs — all taught as chunks with an Uzbek gloss; phrasal verbs and dependent prepositions in context.
ielts.critGrammarWhere: The original B1→B2 grammar spine (two topics a lesson) → POV intuition → 'say a true sentence about yourself' drills: narrative tenses, the conditional ladder, the passive, relative clauses, present-perfect vs past.
ielts.critPronWhere: Shadowing the EnglishPod dialogue and the AJ Hoge MAIN, role-play, Fun English, and minimal-pair self-check on the sounds Uzbek speakers miss.
ielts.readyTitle: Am I ready for a mock test?
ielts.readyLead: A mock (practice) test with a teacher makes sense once most of these feel true:
ielts.ready1: You have finished Phase 3 — all 30 lessons.
ielts.ready2: You are comfortable with the woven Interview-Skills conversations (Lessons 4, 18, 19, 26, 27, 29) that climax at the Lesson 30 capstone.
ielts.ready3: You can speak for about two minutes on a Part 2 topic without long pauses.
ielts.ready4: You can keep a Part 3 discussion going, giving reasons and examples.
ielts.ready5: Your mini-story answers come out automatically — you no longer translate in your head first.
ielts.bridgeBody: Six EnglishPod 'Interview Skills' dialogues are woven through Lessons 4, 18, 19, 26, 27 and 29, and climax at the Lesson 30 capstone. Together they are your bridge from this course to a real, paid mock interview with a teacher.
ielts.badgesNote: CEFR badges unlock at each phase boundary — A2 ✓ → B1 ✓ → B2 in progress — so you can see yourself climb the ladder.
gref.lead: A place to look things up — not a drill. Every grammar topic is taught inside its lesson; this page just helps you find it again.
gref.irregLead: These verbs don't take the regular -ed ending. They're grouped by pattern so they're easier to remember — the past (V2) and past participle (V3) are what to learn.
gref.irregG1Note: One spelling for all three forms. 'read' is written the same, but its past is said like 'red'.
gref.irregG2Note: The past and the past participle share the same form — learn them as a pair.
gref.irregG3Note: All three forms differ — often it is the vowel that changes (begin–began–begun; write–wrote–written).
gref.irregG4Note: The everyday verbs worth memorising first — they follow no tidy pattern.
gref.spellLead: The endings that change a word's spelling. Learn the rule once, then trust it.
gref.sp1Body: Usually just add -s. Add -es after -s, -ss, -sh, -ch, -x and -o. A consonant + y becomes -ies (but a vowel + y just adds -s).
gref.sp2Body: Drop a silent -e. In a short, stressed 'vowel + consonant' word, double the last consonant. -ie becomes -ying.
gref.sp3Body: Add -ed; after -e just add -d. A consonant + y becomes -ied. In a short, stressed 'vowel + consonant' word, double the last consonant.
gref.sp4Body: Short adjectives add -er / -est (and y → -ier / -iest; double a final consonant after a short vowel). Longer adjectives use more / most instead.
gref.sp5Body: A consonant + y usually changes to i before an ending (-es, -ed, -er, -est, -ly). Keep the y before -ing, and after a vowel.
